description: Controlled vocabulary for tags applied to analysis records and extraction journal entries. See .claude/skills/shared/CONVENTIONS.md §14 for rules.

categories.domain: Problem space or field (e.g., knowledge-management, security)
categories.technology: Concrete tech, tools, or protocols (e.g., react, mcp, sqlite)
categories.concept: Named ideas or abstractions (e.g., multi-agent, rag)
categories.pattern: Architectural/design patterns with replicable shape (e.g., plugin-dispatch)
categories.taxonomic: Artifact type (e.g., framework, library) — use sparingly

framework.definition: Opinionated scaffolding with extension points (e.g., Next.js, Django)

library.definition: Focused code module called from consuming projects

research-tool.definition: Tool designed for research workflows rather than production use

curated-list.definition: Human-maintained awesome-list style collection of resources

documentation.definition: Documentation site or reference manual as the primary artifact

blog.definition: Blog or personal site publishing individual posts

utility-tool.definition: Single-purpose utility performing a discrete task

tool-demo.definition: Demonstration of how to use a tool, often stripped-down

example.definition: Example code, cookbook, or illustration of a pattern or approach

paper.definition: Academic paper, preprint, or formal research publication

template.definition: Starter project or boilerplate meant to be copied and customized

claude-code.definition: Anthropic's Claude Code CLI or the Claude model family it uses

obsidian.definition: Obsidian note-taking application and its plugin ecosystem

mcp.definition: Model Context Protocol for connecting AI tools to external systems

llm.definition: Large Language Model as a technology category

tmux.definition: Terminal multiplexer for session and pane management

markdown.definition: Markdown format and its processing tooling

teammatetool.definition: TeammateTool — specific product/project referenced in analyses

composio.definition: Composio SaaS platform for agent tool integration

claude-md.definition: CLAUDE.md file convention for per-project AI instructions

openapi.definition: OpenAPI specification for describing REST APIs

github.definition: GitHub platform (API, Actions, features beyond generic git)

cli.definition: Command-line interface as a deliverable surface

architecture.definition: System architecture as a problem space or concern

security.definition: Security concerns, threat models, and hardening

ci-cd.definition: Continuous integration and continuous deployment pipelines

ai-agents.definition: AI agent development, coordination, and operation

saas.definition: Software-as-a-Service as a business or deployment model

testing.definition: Automated testing, test strategy, and coverage

tool-integration.definition: Integrating external tools into an application

supply-chain.definition: Software supply chain security and dependency provenance

governance.definition: Project or organizational governance, policies, decision-making

ai-integration.definition: Integrating AI capabilities generally into applications

subagents.definition: Claude Code subagents (includes 'agents' in older usage)

personal-wiki.definition: Personal knowledge wiki pattern for individual use

multi-agent.definition: Multiple agents working together, often on parallel tasks

swarm.definition: Swarm-style agent coordination — many agents, emergent behavior

skill.definition: Claude Code skill or similar plugin abstraction

hooks.definition: Hook system for extending runtime behavior (Claude Code hooks, git hooks)

contemplation.definition: Deliberate contemplation or thinking phase in agent design

depth-tiers.definition: Tiered depth levels for analysis or processing (quick/standard/deep)

state-machine.definition: Explicit state-machine pattern for flow control

ai-instructions.definition: Written instructions for AI behavior (e.g., CLAUDE.md files)

mental-model.definition: Explicit mental-model framing for a concept

marketplace.definition: Marketplace pattern for distributing plugins or tools

teams.definition: Agent teams or role-based multi-agent arrangement

extraction.definition: Extracting patterns, content, or structured data from sources

system-prompt.definition: Designing system prompts for model behavior

filtering.definition: Filtering inputs or outputs by criteria

feature-flags.definition: Feature flag system for rollout or experimentation

jason-os-relevant.definition: Directly applicable to the JASON-OS portable Claude Code OS vision

cas-relevant.definition: Directly applicable to the Content Analysis System (T28) work

sonash-relevant.definition: Directly applicable to the SoNash codebase

skill-bloat.definition: Skill system has accumulated excess complexity or breadth

code-bloat.definition: Code has accumulated excess complexity or size

dead-code.definition: Dead or unreachable code in source

commercial-oss.definition: Open-source project with commercial interests or dual licensing

platform-risk.definition: Risk from dependency on a specific platform

hallucination.definition: Source demonstrates or discusses LLM hallucination

best-practices.definition: Source encodes or demonstrates best practices

claims-vs-reality.definition: Source's claims don't match its actual behavior or quality

licensing.definition: Licensing concerns or patterns worth noting

knowledge-concentration.definition: Knowledge concentrated in one place (risk or benefit)

sdk-duplication.definition: Duplicated SDK surfaces across language bindings

premature-optimization.definition: Source contains or demonstrates premature optimization

flakiness.definition: Source demonstrates or discusses test or system flakiness
